informal: Let $F = \mathbb{Z}_7$ and let $p(x) = x^3 - 2$ and $q(x) = x^3 + 2$ be in $F[x]$. Show that $p(x)$ and $q(x)$ are irreducible in $F[x]$ and that the fields $F[x]/(p(x))$ and $F[x]/(q(x))$ are isomorphic.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_4_5_23 {p q: Polynomial (ZMod 7)}
  (hp : p = X^3 - 2) (hq : q = X^3 + 2) :
  Irreducible p ∧ Irreducible q ∧
  (Nonempty $ Polynomial (ZMod 7) ⧸ span ({p} : Set $ Polynomial $ ZMod 7) ≃+*
  Polynomial (ZMod 7) ⧸ span ({q} : Set $ Polynomial $ ZMod 7))
reasoning: The Lean signature takes p = X^3 - 2 and q = X^3 + 2 in (ZMod 7)[X], asserts both are irreducible, and asserts the existence of a ring isomorphism between the quotient rings Polynomial (ZMod 7) ⧸ span {p} and Polynomial (ZMod 7) ⧸ span {q}. Since ZMod 7 = ℤ/7ℤ = F, and span {p} is the ideal generated by p (so the quotient is F[x]/(p)), this matches the informal statement. A ring isomorphism between two fields is the same as a field isomorphism, so ≃+* faithfully encodes "the fields are isomorphic". Nonempty (A ≃+* B) is the standard way to express "there exists an isomorphism".
